title: Mirabilis ovata
intro: Mirabilis ovata, also known as desert four o'clock or American desert four o'clock, is a species of flowering plant in the four o'clock family (Nyctaginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its potential to treat fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis ovata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in the southwestern United States and Mexico.
taxonomy: Mirabilis ovata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales. Nyctaginaceae is a family of plants commonly known as four o'clock flowers or fuchsia family. The family Nyctaginaceae includes around 29 genera and over 500 species of flowering plants. Mirabilis ovata is a species within this family and order, but further classification is needed to identify its exact genus.
uses: Mirabilis ovata, also known as the desert four o'clock, has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds and reducing swelling.
